questions.question: What is the primary purpose of Notion Academy as described in the article?

options.text: To sell premium Notion templates directly to users.
options.explanation: The article does not say Academy sells templates; it focuses on learning.

options.text: To provide structured learning paths, lessons, and badges for mastering Notion.
options.explanation: Correct – the article emphasizes learning paths, lessons, and earning badges.

options.text: To host live webinars exclusively for enterprise customers.
options.explanation: Webinars are mentioned, but they are not the sole purpose of the Academy.

options.text: To replace the Notion Help Center entirely.
options.explanation: The Help Center still exists; Academy supplements it with courses.

questions.question: Which type of credential can you earn by completing a Notion Academy path?

options.explanation: Certification is a separate program; paths grant badges, not the admin cert.
Correct – the article mentions earning badges for completing paths.
No subscription upgrades are offered as a reward.

options.text: A discount on Notion Marketplace templates
options.explanation: Discounts are not listed as a credential.

questions.question: Which lesson is classified as Intermediate and teaches how to build an intake system using forms, charts, and automations?

options.text: An issue tracker for any team
options.explanation: Correct – the description matches the "An issue tracker for any team" lesson.
Project management focuses on tasks, not specifically on intake systems.
Custom Agents are about automation triggers, not an intake system.
Workspace design covers best‑practice layout, not intake forms.

questions.question: How many lessons are included in the Advanced Path?

options.explanation: The Essentials Path has 3 lessons, not the Advanced Path.
The Workflows Path contains 4 lessons.
Correct – the article lists the Advanced Path as having 5 lessons.
No path with 6 lessons is mentioned.

questions.question: Which path is described as helping users "Create connected systems that unite teams and projects, with the help of AI"?

options.explanation: Essentials Path focuses on basic skills, not AI‑enabled connected systems.
Correct – the Workflows Path description matches this wording.
Advanced Path is about automation and architecture, but the quoted phrase belongs to Workflows.
Custom Agents is a lesson, not a path.

questions.question: If a user wants to learn best practices for designing a workspace, which specific lesson should they take, and what is its difficulty level?

options.explanation: Correct – the "Workspace design" lesson (10 min) is labeled Advanced.
Building basics covers navigation and blocks, not workspace design best practices.
That lesson focuses on collaboration, not design.
Templates & marketplace is about creating and selling templates, unrelated to workspace design.

questions.question: Considering the lesson durations listed, which lesson provides the longest dedicated time for learning, and what is its difficulty level?

options.explanation: Correct – at 40 minutes, this is the longest duration mentioned, and it is Intermediate.
Custom Agents is 30 minutes, shorter than Templates & marketplace.

options.text: Connecting knowledge in Notion – Intermediate (20 min)
options.explanation: Only 20 minutes, not the longest.
Only 10 minutes, far shorter than 40 minutes.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about the Notion Certified Admin program is true according to the article?

options.text: It is only available to users on the Enterprise plan.
options.explanation: The article says it is available for any plan type.

options.text: It teaches you how to run Notion at scale, implement security best practices, and gain a recognized credential.
options.explanation: Correct – this matches the description of the Certified Admin program.

options.text: It replaces the need for any other Notion Academy badges.
options.explanation: Certification is separate; badges are still part of the Academy.

options.text: It is a prerequisite for accessing the Notion Help Center.
options.explanation: The Help Center is publicly accessible; certification is not required.
